001package edu.cnm.deepdive.seesomethingabq.controller;
002
003import edu.cnm.deepdive.seesomethingabq.exception.AccessDeniedException;
004import edu.cnm.deepdive.seesomethingabq.exception.BadRequestException;
005import edu.cnm.deepdive.seesomethingabq.exception.ConflictException;
006import edu.cnm.deepdive.seesomethingabq.exception.ResourceNotFoundException;
007import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
008import jakarta.persistence.EntityNotFoundException;
009import jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException;
010import java.time.Instant;
011import java.util.ArrayList;
012import java.util.List;
013import java.util.Map;
014import java.util.NoSuchElementException;
015import java.util.Objects;
016import java.util.Optional;
017import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
018import org.springframework.http.MediaType;
019import org.springframework.http.converter.HttpMessageNotReadableException;
020import org.springframework.web.HttpMediaTypeNotSupportedException;
021import org.springframework.web.method.annotation.MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException;
022import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ExceptionHandler;
023import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ResponseStatus;
024import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestControllerAdvice;
025import org.springframework.core.convert.ConversionFailedException;
026import org.springframework.web.multipart.support.MissingServletRequestPartException;
027import org.springframework.web.servlet.HandlerMapping;
028
029/**
030 * Global exception handler for REST controllers. Maps exceptions to appropriate HTTP status codes.
031 */
032@RestControllerAdvice
033public class GlobalExceptionHandler {
034
035  /**
036   * Simple response payload for error messages returned by this handler.
037   *
038   * @param message error message.
039   * @param timestamp time the error response was created.
040   */
041  public record ErrorResponse(String message, Instant timestamp) {}
042
043  /**
044   * Handles resource not found exceptions.
045   *
046   * @param ex exception being handled.
047   * @return error response payload.
048   */
049  @ExceptionHandler({
050      ResourceNotFoundException.class,
051      NoSuchElementException.class,
052      EntityNotFoundException.class
053  })
054  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND)
055  public ErrorResponse handleNotFound(Exception ex) {
056    return new ErrorResponse(ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
057  }
058
059  /**
060   * Handles access denied exceptions.
061   *
062   * @param ex exception being handled.
063   * @return error response payload.
064   */
065  @ExceptionHandler(AccessDeniedException.class)
066  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)
067  public ErrorResponse handleAccessDenied(AccessDeniedException ex) {
068    return new ErrorResponse(ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
069  }
070
071  /**
072   * Handles bad request exceptions.
073   *
074   * @param ex exception being handled.
075   * @return error response payload.
076   */
077  @ExceptionHandler({
078      IllegalArgumentException.class,
079      BadRequestException.class
080  })
081  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
082  public ErrorResponse handleBadRequest(Exception ex) {
083    return new ErrorResponse(ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
084  }
085
086  /**
087   * Handles Bean Validation failures (e.g., invalid ReportLocation).
088   *
089   * @param ex exception being handled.
090   * @return error response payload.
091   */
092  @ExceptionHandler(ConstraintViolationException.class)
093  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
094  public ErrorResponse handleConstraintViolation(ConstraintViolationException ex) {
095    String message = ex.getConstraintViolations().stream()
096        .map(v -> v.getMessage())
097        .distinct()
098        .reduce((a, b) -> a + "; " + b)
099        .orElse(ex.getMessage());
100    return new ErrorResponse(message, Instant.now());
101  }
102
103  /**
104   * Handles path/query parameter conversion failures (e.g., invalid UUID, invalid int).
105   *
106   * @param ex exception being handled.
107   * @return error response payload.
108   */
109  @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException.class)
110  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
111  public ErrorResponse handleMethodArgumentTypeMismatch(MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException ex) {
112    String name = ex.getName();
113    Object value = ex.getValue();
114    String message = (value != null)
115        ? String.format("Invalid value '%s' for parameter '%s'.", value, name)
116        : String.format("Invalid value for parameter '%s'.", name);
117    return new ErrorResponse(message, Instant.now());
118  }
119
120  /**
121   * Handles low-level conversion failures that may not surface as a {@link MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException}.
122   *
123   * @param ex exception being handled.
124   * @param request HTTP request associated with the failure.
125   * @return error response payload.
126   */
127  @ExceptionHandler(ConversionFailedException.class)
128  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
129  public ErrorResponse handleConversionFailed(ConversionFailedException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
130    Object value = ex.getValue();
131    Optional<String> name = findParameterName(request, value);
132    String message;
133    if (name.isPresent()) {
134      message = (value != null)
135          ? String.format("Invalid value '%s' for parameter '%s'.", value, name.get())
136          : String.format("Invalid value for parameter '%s'.", name.get());
137    } else {
138      message = (value != null)
139          ? String.format("Invalid value '%s'.", value)
140          : "Invalid value.";
141    }
142    return new ErrorResponse(message, Instant.now());
143  }
144
145  /**
146   * Handles malformed/invalid request bodies (e.g., JSON parse errors) as a client error, not a 500.
147   *
148   * @param ex exception being handled.
149   * @return error response payload.
150   */
151  @ExceptionHandler(HttpMessageNotReadableException.class)
152  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
153  public ErrorResponse handleHttpMessageNotReadable(HttpMessageNotReadableException ex) {
154    return new ErrorResponse("Malformed request body.", Instant.now());
155  }
156
157  /**
158   * Handles multipart/form-data requests missing a required part (e.g., missing "file" upload).
159   *
160   * @param ex exception being handled.
161   * @return error response payload.
162   */
163  @ExceptionHandler(MissingServletRequestPartException.class)
164  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST)
165  public ErrorResponse handleMissingServletRequestPart(MissingServletRequestPartException ex) {
166    return new ErrorResponse(ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
167  }
168
169  /**
170   * Handles requests with unsupported media/content types.
171   *
172   * @param ex exception being handled.
173   * @return error response payload.
174   */
175  @ExceptionHandler(HttpMediaTypeNotSupportedException.class)
176  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE)
177  public ErrorResponse handleHttpMediaTypeNotSupported(HttpMediaTypeNotSupportedException ex) {
178    MediaType received = ex.getContentType();
179    String receivedText = (received != null) ? received.toString() : "(none)";
180    List<MediaType> supported = ex.getSupportedMediaTypes();
181    String message = (supported != null && !supported.isEmpty())
182        ? String.format("Unsupported content type '%s'. Supported: %s",
183            receivedText,
184            supported.stream().map(MediaType::toString).distinct().reduce((a, b) -> a + ", " + b).orElse(""))
185        : String.format("Unsupported content type '%s'.", receivedText);
186    return new ErrorResponse(message, Instant.now());
187  }
188
189  /**
190   * Handles conflict exceptions (duplicate types, states, deleting in-use resources, etc.).
191   *
192   * @param ex exception being handled.
193   * @return error response payload.
194   */
195  @ExceptionHandler({
196      ConflictException.class,
197      IllegalStateException.class
198  })
199  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.CONFLICT)
200  public ErrorResponse handleConflict(Exception ex) {
201    return new ErrorResponse(ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
202  }
203
204  /**
205   * Handles all other unhandled exceptions.
206   *
207   * @param ex exception being handled.
208   * @return error response payload.
209   */
210  @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class)
211  @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
212  public ErrorResponse handleInternalError(Exception ex) {
213    return new ErrorResponse("An unexpected error occurred: " + ex.getMessage(), Instant.now());
214  }
215
216  /**
217   * Attempts to find the parameter name associated with a conversion failure by uniquely matching the
218   * failed value against query parameters and path variables. If matching is ambiguous or impossible,
219   * returns empty rather than guessing.
220   */
221  private Optional<String> findParameterName(HttpServletRequest request, Object value) {
222    if (request == null || value == null) {
223      return Optional.empty();
224    }
225    String raw = Objects.toString(value, null);
226    if (raw == null) {
227      return Optional.empty();
228    }
229    List<String> matches = new ArrayList<>();
230
231    request.getParameterMap().forEach((name, values) -> {
232      if (values != null) {
233        for (String v : values) {
234          if (raw.equals(v)) {
235            matches.add(name);
236            return;
237          }
238        }
239      }
240    });
241
242    Object attr = request.getAttribute(HandlerMapping.URI_TEMPLATE_VARIABLES_ATTRIBUTE);
243    if (attr instanceof Map<?, ?> vars) {
244      vars.forEach((k, v) -> {
245        if (k != null && v != null && raw.equals(v.toString())) {
246          matches.add(k.toString());
247        }
248      });
249    }
250
251    return (matches.size() == 1) ? Optional.of(matches.getFirst()) : Optional.empty();
252  }
253}
